subjective.chief_complaint: Fever and not herself
history_of_present_illness: 2-month-old infant
Felt warm mid-morning, became really hot in afternoon
Temperature 38.7°C measured at home with ear thermometer (checked twice)
More sleepy than usual, keeps dozing off during feeds
Reduced oral intake: taking approximately 50-60mls per feed compared to usual 120mls
Reduced urine output: only 2 wet nappies today compared to usual 4-5
One episode of small vomit earlier today
relevant_history: Vaccinations up to date (6-week immunisations completed on time)

observed_general_state: Appears floppy compared to usual per parent report

assessment: Febrile infant under 3 months requiring urgent medical review
Reduced feeding and urine output suggesting early dehydration
plan: Priority urgent assessment - bypass waiting room
Direct admission to paediatric assessment area
Likely septic workup: blood tests, urine collection, and other investigations as determined by medical officer
Admission likely given age and presentation

brief_summary: Aria is a 2-month-old baby with a fever of 38.5°C who is sleepier than usual, feeding poorly, and has fewer wet nappies. Because she is so young and has a fever, she needs to be seen by a doctor urgently and will likely have some tests done and be admitted to hospital.

subjective.chief_complaint: Wheezy and struggling to breathe
history_of_present_illness: 3-year-old male with worsening respiratory symptoms since lunchtime
Cold for couple of days prior
This afternoon developed fast breathing and subcostal retractions
Given 6 puffs Ventolin in last hour with minimal improvement
Fever 38.3°C in afternoon, Panadol administered
Poor oral intake, too breathless to drink properly

assessment: Inadequate response to home bronchodilator therapy
plan: Immediate transfer to acute care area, no waiting room
Medical assessment to determine need for admission versus discharge after stabilisation

brief_summary: Noah is a 3-year-old with known asthma who is having trouble breathing today. His oxygen level is low and his home puffer hasn't helped much. He needs urgent treatment with breathing medications right away and the doctors will decide if he needs to stay in hospital.

subjective.chief_complaint: Vomiting and diarrhoea since yesterday
history_of_present_illness: 4-year-old female with vomiting and diarrhoea onset yesterday
At least 8 episodes of vomiting since yesterday morning, mostly today
7-8 episodes of watery diarrhoea
Tolerating only tiny sips of fluid, sometimes vomits immediately after
Last urination this morning, none since (reduced from usual frequency)
Fever 38°C last night, treated with paracetamol
Attempted water and Hydralyte, vomited Hydralyte
Intermittent abdominal pain reported by patient
associated_symptoms: Increased lethargy, lying quietly on parent
relevant_history: Brother had gastroenteritis last week, less severe illness

plan: Transfer to paediatric acute area for medical assessment
Intravenous fluids may be required if unable to tolerate oral intake

brief_summary: Lily is a 4-year-old with vomiting and diarrhoea since yesterday who is showing signs of moderate dehydration. She will be seen quickly in the paediatric area where doctors will assess her and likely start fluids, either by mouth or through a drip if needed.

subjective.chief_complaint: Head injury from fall off monkey bars
history_of_present_illness: 9-year-old male fell off monkey bars at school approximately 1 hour prior to presentation
Struck back of head
Cried immediately after fall
Reports mild headache

observed_general_state: Alert and oriented to person and place

assessment: Minor head injury with no red flags
No indication for CT scan based on presentation
plan: CT scan unlikely based on current presentation

brief_summary: Max is a 9-year-old boy who fell off monkey bars and hit the back of his head about an hour ago. He has a mild headache but is acting normally with no worrying signs. He will be seen by a doctor and observed in the minor injuries area.

subjective.chief_complaint: Laceration to forearm from metal object
history_of_present_illness: 7-year-old female slipped and cut forearm on metal approximately 40 minutes ago
Initial heavy bleeding controlled with direct pressure
relevant_history: Vaccinations up to date including tetanus

exam_findings: Forearm laceration approximately 3 cm in length
Not actively bleeding at time of examination

assessment: Forearm laceration likely requiring closure with sutures or tissue adhesive
plan: Patient to be seen in minor treatment area
Local anaesthetic to be administered prior to wound closure
Awaiting medical assessment for definitive wound closure

brief_summary: Sophie, a 7-year-old girl, cut her forearm on metal about 40 minutes ago. The bleeding has stopped and the wound is stable but will likely need stitches or glue. She will be seen in the minor treatment area after a short wait.

subjective.chief_complaint: Left wrist injury following fall from skateboard
history_of_present_illness: 10-year-old male fell off skateboard approximately 1 hour ago
Injured wrist
Reports significant pain with movement

exam_findings: Good circulation - fingers warm and pink

plan: Likely cast application depending on X-ray findings

brief_summary: Jai, a 10-year-old boy, fell off his skateboard about an hour ago and hurt his wrist. The nurse found swelling and tenderness but good circulation and nerve function. He will get an X-ray soon and may need a cast depending on the results.

subjective.chief_complaint: Abdominal pain since yesterday, worsening today
history_of_present_illness: 11-year-old male with abdominal pain onset yesterday
Pain initially periumbilical, now localized to right lower quadrant
Pain worse today
Pain worsens with walking

exam_findings: Tender in right lower quadrant on palpation

plan: Surgical team consultation if appendicitis confirmed

brief_summary: Leo is an 11-year-old boy with worsening tummy pain that moved from his belly button to the lower right side, along with vomiting and fever. This could be appendicitis. He will be admitted to the children's ward for tests and review by doctors, and may need surgery if appendicitis is confirmed.

subjective.chief_complaint: Accidental peanut ingestion with allergic reaction
history_of_present_illness: 6-year-old female accidentally ate peanuts approximately 20 minutes prior to presentation
EpiPen administered by parent 10 minutes ago
Symptoms began after ingestion
relevant_history: Known peanut allergy (implied by EpiPen availability)

assessment: Active anaphylaxis with airway involvement following peanut ingestion
plan: May require additional medication for ongoing anaphylaxis
Ongoing monitoring and medical assessment in resus

brief_summary: Ava, a 6-year-old girl, accidentally ate peanuts and is having a serious allergic reaction with swelling, throat tightness, and breathing difficulty. Her parent gave her an EpiPen, which was the right thing to do. She is being taken immediately to the resuscitation area for urgent treatment and may need more medication.

subjective.chief_complaint: Seizure at home with fever
history_of_present_illness: 3-year-old child presented following first-ever seizure at home
Seizure occurred approximately 10 minutes prior to arrival
Duration approximately 2 minutes
Generalised tonic-clonic seizure involving arms and legs
Cyanosis of lips noted during seizure
Fever present since afternoon
Seizure has ceased, child now post-ictal

observed_general_state: Sleepy but appropriate for post-seizure period

assessment: ATS Category 2 - requires urgent medical assessment
plan: Immediate transfer to acute paediatrics for urgent assessment
Medical evaluation for first febrile seizure

brief_summary: A 3-year-old child had their first seizure at home today, lasting about 2 minutes, along with a fever. The seizure has stopped and the child is sleepy, which is normal afterward. They are being taken straight to the pediatric team for urgent assessment.

subjective.chief_complaint: Suspected coin ingestion
history_of_present_illness: 2-year-old male swallowed coin approximately 30 minutes prior to presentation
Witnessed by sibling who saw patient put coin in mouth
Initial coughing episode at time of ingestion
Coughing has since resolved

exam_findings: Chest auscultation: equal air entry bilaterally

assessment: Foreign body ingestion (coin), airway secure, stable presentation
plan: X-ray to locate coin and assess for oesophageal impaction
Transfer to paediatric area for further assessment

brief_summary: Oliver is a 2-year-old boy who swallowed a coin about 30 minutes ago. He is breathing normally and is stable. An X-ray will be done to find out where the coin is and what needs to happen next.

summary_header: Mental Health Crisis / Suicidal Ideation - 22-year-old Male (ATS 2)

subjective.chief_complaint: Suicidal ideation with safety concerns
history_of_present_illness: Patient reports active thoughts about ending his life
States thoughts keep coming into his head and is scared he might act on them
Did not feel safe at home
Self-harmed by cutting arm earlier today, described as not deep
Consumed significant amount of alcohol today
Stopped taking antidepressant medications a few weeks ago
negatives: No illicit drug use (no cannabis, pills, or methamphetamine)
relevant_history: Recently discontinued psychiatric medications (few weeks ago)

exam_findings: Self-inflicted laceration to arm, described as superficial
observed_general_state: Tachycardia noted, attributed to alcohol consumption and anxiety

assessment: Active suicidal ideation with recent self-harm
plan: Immediate placement in safe, private room
No waiting room placement due to safety concerns

brief_summary: A 22-year-old man came to the emergency department because he is having thoughts of ending his life and does not feel safe. He cut his arm and drank alcohol earlier today. He will be placed in a safe private room and seen urgently by the doctor and mental health team.

summary_header: Lower Abdominal Pain - Possible Ectopic Pregnancy - 23-year-old (ATS 3)

subjective.chief_complaint: Really bad lower tummy pain
history_of_present_illness: Pain started this morning
Pain has gotten much worse in the last couple of hours
Pain mostly on the right side, sharp in nature
One week late for period
Some vaginal spotting earlier
Pregnancy possible
negatives: No dizziness or feeling faint (only mild light-headedness)

assessment: Lower abdominal pain with delayed menses and vaginal spotting
Possible ectopic pregnancy to be ruled out

brief_summary: A 23-year-old woman came in with worsening right-sided lower abdominal pain, a late period, and some spotting. Because of concern for a possible ectopic pregnancy, she will have a pregnancy test, blood work, and be seen by a doctor soon.

subjective.chief_complaint: Twisted ankle playing soccer approximately 2 hours ago
history_of_present_illness: Injury occurred during soccer
Popping sensation felt at time of injury
Able to bear some weight but with significant pain
Significant swelling around outside of ankle since injury
negatives: No numbness or tingling in foot

assessment: Ankle injury - suspected sprain versus fracture
plan: X-ray required to differentiate sprain from fracture
To be seen in minor injuries area

brief_summary: A 19-year-old male twisted his ankle playing soccer two hours ago and heard a pop. There is swelling and pain, but circulation is good. He will need an X-ray to check if the ankle is broken or sprained.

subjective.chief_complaint: Alcohol and oxycodone ingestion, feeling really out of it
history_of_present_illness: Ingested 6-7 oxycodone tablets approximately 1 hour prior to presentation
Consumed large amount of vodka
Oxycodone was partner's medication, not prescribed to patient
Patient does not normally take oxycodone for pain
Denies suicidal intent, states was not thinking
negatives: No trouble breathing reported by patient

assessment: Polysubstance intoxication with alcohol and oxycodone
plan: Immediate transfer to acute area for close monitoring

brief_summary: A 25-year-old male came to the emergency department after drinking a large amount of alcohol and taking 6-7 oxycodone tablets about an hour ago. He is feeling very sleepy and his breathing is slightly slow. He will be taken to the acute care area right away for close monitoring because this combination can cause dangerous slowing of breathing.

subjective.chief_complaint: Shortness of breath
history_of_present_illness: Known asthma
Shortness of breath since this morning, worsening
Used Ventolin 6 puffs over last hour with minimal relief
Cough for 2 days
associated_symptoms: Difficulty speaking in full sentences (feels harder than usual)
relevant_history: One previous hospitalisation for asthma as a child

observed_general_state: Breathing fast but not severely distressed
Able to speak in full sentences

brief_summary: A 20-year-old woman with asthma is experiencing worsening shortness of breath since this morning that hasn't improved much with her inhaler. She will be taken to acute care soon for breathing treatments and medication to help open her airways.

subjective.chief_complaint: Worst headache of my life with sudden onset approximately one hour ago
history_of_present_illness: Sudden onset thunderclap headache starting approximately 1 hour prior to presentation
Instantaneous onset described as 'like someone hit me with something'
Neck stiffness present with pain on head movement
One episode of vomiting
Patient reports feeling 'really off'
negatives: No weakness in arms or legs

assessment: Thunderclap headache with red flag features: sudden onset, neck stiffness, vomiting
Possible subarachnoid hemorrhage requiring urgent investigation
plan: Likely investigations to rule out intracranial hemorrhage

brief_summary: A 28-year-old woman came in with a sudden, severe headache that started instantly about an hour ago, along with neck stiffness and vomiting. Because these symptoms could indicate bleeding in the brain, she will be seen immediately by the medical team for urgent testing.

subjective.chief_complaint: Swollen and red leg
history_of_present_illness: Started as a small cut two days ago
Redness spreading up calf
Small amount of pus or discharge present

brief_summary: A 27-year-old woman came in with a swollen, red leg that started from a small cut two days ago. The redness is spreading and she had chills last night, suggesting an infection. She will have blood tests done and will be started on antibiotics.

subjective.chief_complaint: Fainted at work with blackout
history_of_present_illness: Patient reports syncopal episode at work
Prodromal symptoms of light-headedness and diaphoresis
Did not hit head, was caught by someone during collapse

brief_summary: A 21-year-old male fainted at work after feeling light-headed and sweaty. He did not injure himself and his vital signs are mostly stable, though his heart rate is a bit fast. He will have an ECG and blood tests done to find out why he fainted.

summary_header: Renal Colic - Severe Flank Pain - 26-year-old Male (ATS 3)

subjective.chief_complaint: Worst pain in right side, comes in waves
history_of_present_illness: Severe right-sided flank pain
Onset 2 hours ago
Pain described as coming in waves
Pain severity 10/10

brief_summary: A 26-year-old man came to the emergency department with severe pain in his right side that started 2 hours ago. He also has burning when urinating and noticed pink urine. He will receive pain medication and have tests done to check for kidney stones.

subjective.chief_complaint: Assault by partner with punch to ribs and face
history_of_present_illness: Patient reports being hit by partner tonight
Punched in ribs and face
Significant rib pain on breathing
relevant_history: Does not feel safe to return home

observed_general_state: Patient requested private room for discussion

assessment: Assault victim with rib and facial trauma
Safety concerns - patient does not feel safe to return home
plan: Medical evaluation for rib and facial injuries

brief_summary: A 24-year-old woman came to the emergency department after being assaulted by her partner. She has pain in her ribs and face and does not feel safe going home. She will be seen by the medical team and connected with social work and support services to ensure her safety.

subjective.chief_complaint: Chest pain
history_of_present_illness: Chest pain started approximately 30 minutes ago while watching TV
Sudden onset
Central chest location
Described as tight
Radiating to left arm
First episode of chest pain of this nature
negatives: No previous chest pain like this
relevant_history: No analgesia taken prior to presentation

assessment: Chest pain with red-flag features concerning for acute coronary syndrome
plan: Transfer to acute area for urgent assessment

brief_summary: A 54-year-old woman came to the emergency department with sudden chest pain that started 30 minutes ago. The pain is in the center of her chest, feels tight, spreads to her left arm, and she is sweating and short of breath. She will be taken to the acute area right away for an ECG and urgent heart checks.

summary_header: Right Upper Quadrant Abdominal Pain - Possible Cholecystitis - 45-year-old Female (ATS 3)

subjective.chief_complaint: Bad pain under right ribs since last night, getting worse
history_of_present_illness: Pain started after dinner yesterday evening
Ate fried chicken and chips prior to onset
Pain has not resolved
Pain radiates to back under right shoulder blade
Felt hot overnight
negatives: No jaundice (no yellowing of eyes or skin)

exam_findings: Right upper quadrant tenderness on palpation

assessment: Right upper quadrant abdominal pain with fever and vomiting
plan: To be seen in acute area

brief_summary: A 45-year-old woman came in with worsening pain under her right ribs that started after eating a fatty meal last night. The pain spreads to her back, and she has vomited and felt feverish. She will have blood tests and possibly an ultrasound to check her gallbladder, and will be seen in the acute care area.

summary_header: Shortness of Breath - COPD Exacerbation - 61-year-old Male (ATS 2)

subjective.chief_complaint: Shortness of breath since this morning, getting worse
history_of_present_illness: Shortness of breath started this morning
Progressive worsening throughout the day
Productive cough with greenish phlegm for a few days
Subjective fevers with hot and cold sensations
Chest tightness without pain
Increased inhaler use today

assessment: Acute dyspnoea with hypoxia in patient with known COPD
Likely acute exacerbation of COPD with possible infective component
plan: Transfer to acute area for immediate medical assessment

brief_summary: A 61-year-old man with COPD came in with worsening shortness of breath and a cough producing green phlegm. His oxygen level is low at 90%. He will be given oxygen and breathing treatments right away in the acute care area.

summary_header: Worsening Headache - Atypical Migraine vs Red Flag - 37-year-old Female (ATS 2)

subjective.chief_complaint: Headache for three days, worsening, different from usual migraines
history_of_present_illness: Headache present for 3 days
Progressive worsening
Atypical pattern compared to usual migraines
Pain described as throbbing across whole head
negatives: No weakness in arms or legs

vitals.note: Pulse and blood pressure checked, reported as okay

assessment: Atypical worsening headache with change in pattern from usual migraines
Possible red flag features: atypical presentation, neck stiffness, visual changes, progressive worsening

brief_summary: A 37-year-old woman came in with a three-day headache that is getting worse and feels different from her usual migraines. She also has some blurry vision, nausea, and mild neck stiffness. She will be assessed urgently and have some tests done to find out what's causing this change.

summary_header: Acute Back Pain - No Red Flags - 40-year-old Male (ATS 4)

subjective.chief_complaint: Lower back pain following lifting injury
history_of_present_illness: Injured lower back lifting boxes yesterday
Pain is really painful today
Pain located in lower back, mostly on right side
Difficulty walking due to pain
negatives: No numbness or tingling in legs

exam_findings: Limited range of motion - patient bends slightly and winces

brief_summary: A 40-year-old male hurt his lower back yesterday while lifting boxes and is experiencing significant pain today. The nurse found tenderness and limited movement but no serious warning signs. He will receive pain medication and be seen by a doctor soon.

summary_header: Fall + Wrist Deformity - Possible Fracture - 48-year-old Female (ATS 3)

subjective.chief_complaint: Wrist injury following fall at work
history_of_present_illness: Slipped at work and landed on hand approximately 1 hour ago
Wrist is really swollen
Very painful
Can barely move fingers

assessment: Likely wrist fracture with obvious deformity

brief_summary: A 48-year-old woman fell at work and injured her wrist about an hour ago. The wrist appears deformed and is very likely broken. She will have an X-ray and receive pain medication.

subjective.chief_complaint: Room spinning since this morning, especially with head movement
history_of_present_illness: Vertigo onset this morning
Symptoms worse with head movement

observed_general_state: Patient able to walk despite dizziness

brief_summary: A 34-year-old woman came in with room spinning and dizziness that started this morning, worse when she moves her head. She also has ringing in her left ear and had a cold last week. She will be assessed by a doctor to confirm the cause and discuss treatment.

subjective.chief_complaint: Red, hot, spreading rash on calf
history_of_present_illness: Started as a scratch 2 days ago
Now red and hot
Spreading up calf
Some swelling present

assessment: Cellulitis with systemic features in diabetic patient

brief_summary: A 59-year-old man with diabetes has a spreading infection on his leg that started from a scratch two days ago. He has fever and the area is red, hot, and swollen. He will receive antibiotics through an IV and have blood tests done.

subjective.chief_complaint: Vomited blood approximately one hour ago
history_of_present_illness: Haematemesis one hour prior to presentation
Dark red blood
Volume sufficient to partially fill toilet bowl
Black stools for two days

assessment: Upper gastrointestinal bleed with haematemesis and melaena
plan: Likely investigations: full blood count, coagulation studies, type and crossmatch, venous access

brief_summary: A 52-year-old man came to the emergency department after vomiting a large amount of dark red blood. He has low blood pressure and a fast heart rate, indicating he needs urgent treatment. He is being taken immediately to the resuscitation area for emergency care.

subjective.chief_complaint: Unable to pass urine since last night with sensation of bladder fullness
history_of_present_illness: Unable to urinate since last night
Bladder feels like it is going to burst
Small amount of blood in urine yesterday

exam_findings: Palpable distended bladder on lower abdominal examination
Significant tenderness on palpation of lower abdomen

assessment: Acute urinary retention, likely secondary to benign prostatic hyperplasia
plan: Urinary catheterization required to relieve retention

brief_summary: A 63-year-old man came in because he has not been able to urinate since last night and his bladder is very full and uncomfortable. He has a history of an enlarged prostate. A catheter will be inserted to drain his bladder and relieve the discomfort.

subjective.chief_complaint: Right hip pain following fall in bathroom this morning
history_of_present_illness: Patient fell in bathroom this morning
Reports feeling weak with legs giving way prior to fall
Right hip pain, severe
Unable to move right leg due to pain

exam_findings: Right leg shortened and externally rotated

assessment: Suspected fractured neck of femur (hip fracture) right side
plan: Transfer to acute care for immediate assessment

brief_summary: An 82-year-old woman fell at home this morning and has severe right hip pain. Her leg position suggests a possible hip fracture. She will receive pain relief and X-rays right away, and the doctors will assess whether surgery is needed.

subjective.chief_complaint: Acute confusion since this morning
history_of_present_illness: 78-year-old male presented by relative with acute onset confusion noted upon waking this morning
Patient did not recognise where he was
Fall yesterday, patient reported feeling fine afterwards
Reports stinging on urination
relevant_history: Previous episode of confusion associated with UTI last year

assessment: Possible head injury related to recent fall
plan: Likely investigations: urinalysis, blood work, consideration of head imaging given recent fall

brief_summary: A 78-year-old man arrived with sudden confusion that started this morning. He had a fall yesterday and is reporting painful urination, with a fever and fast heart rate noted. He will be seen urgently by the doctor to check for infection or injury from the fall.

summary_header: Abdominal Pain - Possible Bowel Obstruction - 74-year-old Female (ATS 3)

subjective.chief_complaint: Abdominal pain since last night
history_of_present_illness: Crampy abdominal pain since last night
Pain described as feeling full
Pain located all over abdomen, worse around the middle
Vomited twice this morning
Last bowel motion yesterday morning, nothing since
Last passed wind yesterday morning

brief_summary: A 74-year-old woman came in with stomach pain, vomiting, and no bowel movements since yesterday. The nurse found her belly swollen and tender, and suspects a possible blockage in her bowel. She will have X-rays and a CT scan done and be moved to the acute care area for further treatment.

summary_header: Shortness of Breath - Heart Failure Exacerbation - 79-year-old Male (ATS 2)

subjective.chief_complaint: Sudden onset shortness of breath
history_of_present_illness: Sudden onset of severe shortness of breath this afternoon
Increased leg swelling this week, more than usual
Weight gain this week
Increased fluid retention

assessment: Acute dyspnoea with hypoxia in patient with known congestive heart failure
Likely heart failure exacerbation given increased leg swelling, weight gain, and fluid retention
plan: Urgent transfer to acute care area

brief_summary: A 79-year-old man came to the emergency department with sudden severe shortness of breath and low oxygen levels. He has a history of heart failure and has noticed increased swelling and weight gain this week. He is being given oxygen and taken immediately to the acute care area for urgent treatment and monitoring.

summary_header: Atypical Chest Pain / Possible ACS - 73-year-old Female (ATS 2)

subjective.chief_complaint: Feeling weak and breathless with pressure in chest but not really pain
history_of_present_illness: Symptoms started a couple of hours ago
Describes chest pressure rather than pain
Associated weakness and breathlessness

assessment: Atypical presentation concerning for acute coronary syndrome in elderly patient with previous MI
plan: Transfer to acute area for immediate assessment

brief_summary: A 73-year-old woman came in with chest pressure, weakness, and shortness of breath that started a couple hours ago. She had a heart attack five years ago, so the team is taking her to the acute area right away for an ECG and heart monitoring to check if this is another heart problem.

summary_header: Fall With Laceration - On Anticoagulants - 85-year-old Female (ATS 3)

subjective.chief_complaint: Tripped over rug and cut arm
history_of_present_illness: Mechanical trip over rug
Sustained laceration to arm

assessment: Laceration to arm in patient on anticoagulation therapy
Requires coagulation studies prior to repair
plan: Blood tests to check clotting status

brief_summary: An 85-year-old woman tripped on a rug and cut her arm. Because she takes a blood thinner, blood tests are needed before stitching the wound. She is stable and will be moved to the treatment area shortly.

subjective.chief_complaint: Sleepy and confused with strong-smelling urine
history_of_present_illness: 76-year-old female presenting with altered mental status
Increased sleepiness and confusion
Strong-smelling urine noted by relative
Fever reported last night
Dysuria reported yesterday

assessment: Suspected serious infection from urinary tract origin

brief_summary: A 76-year-old woman came in feeling sleepy and confused with a fever and signs of a urinary infection. Her vital signs suggest a serious infection that may have spread to her bloodstream. She will be taken immediately to the acute area for blood tests, fluids through an IV, and antibiotics.

summary_header: GI Bleed - Black Stools / Dizziness - 81-year-old Male (ATS 2)

subjective.chief_complaint: Dark stools for two days and feeling faint
history_of_present_illness: Very dark stools for 2 days
Feeling faint

assessment: Suspected gastrointestinal bleed with haemodynamic changes in patient on anticoagulation
plan: Urgent care - patient to be taken straight through
Awaiting medical assessment for management of suspected GI bleed

brief_summary: An 81-year-old man has been passing very dark stools for two days and feels faint. He is on blood thinners and his blood pressure is low with a fast heart rate, suggesting possible bleeding in the digestive system. He will be seen urgently by the medical team.

subjective.chief_complaint: Non-healing wound on shin with redness and pain
history_of_present_illness: Wound on shin present for a few weeks
Wound has not been healing
Redness has spread over the last two days
Area is painful

brief_summary: A 72-year-old man has a wound on his shin that hasn't healed for a few weeks and has become red, swollen, and infected over the past two days. He will have blood tests and a wound swab taken, and antibiotics will be started to treat the infection.

subjective.chief_complaint: Decreased oral intake for several days
history_of_present_illness: 88-year-old male with poor oral intake (food and fluids) for days
Relative reports patient has been confused

plan: Monitoring of vital signs and clinical status

brief_summary: An 88-year-old man has not been eating or drinking well for several days and is now confused. His blood pressure is low and he is dehydrated. He will be admitted to receive fluids through an IV and have blood tests done.

summary_header: Early Pregnancy + Lower Abdominal Pain - Possible Ectopic - 7 Weeks Pregnant (ATS 2)

subjective.chief_complaint: Lower abdominal pain at 7 weeks gestation
history_of_present_illness: 7 weeks pregnant
Lower abdominal pain started this afternoon
Pain worsening
Pain predominantly right-sided
Sharp pain
Vaginal spotting, not heavy, noted on wiping
associated_symptoms: Nausea and vomiting (attributed to morning sickness)
relevant_history: No ultrasound performed yet this pregnancy

assessment: Early pregnancy (7 weeks gestation) with unilateral lower abdominal pain and vaginal spotting
Possible ectopic pregnancy - requires urgent investigation
plan: Blood tests including serum beta-hCG (pregnancy hormone level)
Monitor for signs of haemodynamic instability

brief_summary: A pregnant woman at 7 weeks is experiencing worsening right-sided lower abdominal pain and light vaginal spotting. She will have urgent blood tests and an ultrasound to check for a possible ectopic pregnancy, which needs immediate attention.

subjective.chief_complaint: Vaginal bleeding at 22 weeks gestation
history_of_present_illness: 22 weeks pregnant
Vaginal bleeding started approximately 1 hour ago
Bleeding sufficient to soak a pad
Bleeding ongoing but possibly decreasing in amount
Mild cramping present, not severe
relevant_history: No complications in pregnancy to date

brief_summary: A woman who is 22 weeks pregnant came in with vaginal bleeding that started about an hour ago. The bleeding soaked a pad and is still happening with mild cramping. She will go directly to the acute area for monitoring, ultrasound, and blood tests with the obstetrics team.

subjective.chief_complaint: Burning on urination and urinary frequency every 10 minutes
history_of_present_illness: Symptoms started 3 days ago
Worsening today
Dysuria present
Urinary frequency approximately every 10 minutes
Pink-tinged urine noted today with a couple of drops
relevant_history: Previous history of UTIs several years ago

brief_summary: A 30-year-old patient came in with burning during urination and needing to urinate very frequently for the past 3 days. This appears to be a straightforward bladder infection. The patient will provide a urine sample and be seen by a doctor shortly.

summary_header: Postpartum Mental Health Crisis - Suicidal Thoughts - 4 Weeks Postpartum (ATS 2)

subjective.chief_complaint: Not coping 4 weeks postpartum with thoughts of self-harm
history_of_present_illness: 4 weeks postpartum
Reports not coping
Active suicidal ideation present at time of assessment
Experiencing intrusive thoughts about harming baby
Patient expresses fear about these thoughts
States does not want to feel this way
relevant_history: Partner at home described as supportive but exhausted
Has not disclosed full extent of symptoms to partner

assessment: Postpartum mental health crisis with active suicidal ideation
Intrusive thoughts of harm to infant
plan: Immediate placement in safe, private room

brief_summary: A mother who gave birth 4 weeks ago is having thoughts of hurting herself and is very scared. She has not acted on these thoughts and came in for help. She will be taken immediately to a safe private room where a mental health team will see her right away.

summary_header: Headache + Visual Changes - 32 Weeks Pregnant - Possible Pre-eclampsia (ATS 2)

subjective.chief_complaint: Severe headache and blurry vision at 32 weeks gestation
history_of_present_illness: 32 weeks pregnant
Headache started this morning, now much worse
Blurry vision
Right upper quadrant pain present
Swelling in hands and face described as 'really puffy'
relevant_history: No known blood pressure issues in pregnancy until now

assessment: Possible pre-eclampsia at 32 weeks gestation with elevated blood pressure, severe headache, visual disturbance, right upper quadrant pain, and edema
plan: ATS Category 2 - immediate medical review required

brief_summary: A pregnant woman at 32 weeks is experiencing a severe headache, blurry vision, swelling, and high blood pressure. These symptoms suggest a serious pregnancy complication called pre-eclampsia. She will be taken immediately to the maternal care area for urgent assessment by the obstetrics team.

subjective.chief_complaint: Left breast pain and redness
history_of_present_illness: 29-year-old breastfeeding patient
Left breast pain for 2 days, worsening today
Painful hard area on upper part of left breast
Fever this morning
Chills last night
Unable to breastfeed from affected side due to pain

exam_findings: Warm, red area on left breast

plan: To be seen in acute care shortly

brief_summary: A breastfeeding mother has a painful, red, swollen area on her left breast with fever and chills for two days. This is likely an infection called mastitis. She will be seen shortly in acute care and will need antibiotics and pain medication.

subjective.chief_complaint: Left wrist pain following fall
history_of_present_illness: Missed a step and fell forward
Landed on left wrist
Reports severe pain in left wrist
relevant_history: Known osteoporosis, described as quite bad

plan: X-ray left wrist to confirm fracture
Likely immobilization with cast pending X-ray results

brief_summary: A 68-year-old patient fell and injured their left wrist. The nurse found swelling and deformity suggesting a likely fracture. The patient will have an X-ray, receive pain medication, and be moved to acute care for further treatment.

subjective.chief_complaint: Feeling faint, especially when standing up
history_of_present_illness: Heavy menstrual periods for the last few months
Current cycle requiring pad change every hour for two days
Passing lots of clots
associated_symptoms: Shortness of breath with stairs lately

assessment: Possible anaemia secondary to heavy menstrual bleeding
Symptomatic with postural dizziness and tachycardia
plan: Transfer to acute care for further assessment and management

brief_summary: A 34-year-old woman came in feeling faint when standing up and has been having very heavy periods with clots for several months. She also gets short of breath with stairs. Blood tests will be done urgently and she will be seen in acute care to check for anaemia and manage her symptoms.

subjective.chief_complaint: Itchy rash on both arms
history_of_present_illness: Rash appeared yesterday after gardening
Possible exposure to weeds
Uncertain about contact with new plants or chemicals
negatives: No swelling of lips or eyes

exam_findings: Red, raised patches on both arms consistent with contact dermatitis

brief_summary: A 27-year-old patient came in with an itchy rash on both arms that started after gardening yesterday. The nurse found it looks like contact dermatitis and the patient is stable. Treatment will include antihistamines and steroid cream.

subjective.chief_complaint: Vomiting since last night and high blood sugars
history_of_present_illness: Vomiting since last night
Home blood glucose 25 mmol/L
Constant abdominal ache
Mild shortness of breath
Recent cold illness
Has not been eating
Skipped insulin yesterday

brief_summary: A 38-year-old with diabetes has been vomiting and has very high blood sugar levels after missing insulin doses while sick with a cold. The nurse suspects a serious complication called diabetic ketoacidosis and is moving the patient immediately to the acute area for blood tests, fluids, and insulin treatment.
